passages.passage_text: Each of the following questions consists of five problem figures marked as 1, 2, 3, 4 and 5, followed by five answer figures marked as (a), (b), (c), (d) and (e). If one of the five answer figures is placed in the place of '?' in the problem figure, a series is established and the figure which replaces the '?' is your answer.

solutions.solution: In each step, one of the circles turns black and moves to a corner of the square boundary.
The shading moves one, two, three, four, ... spaces anti-clockwise sequentially.
The number of sides of the figure reduces by one in each step.
Half portion of a rectangle is lost in one step and one complete rectangle is lost in the next step.
The circle moves half-a-side of the square boundary in clockwise direction in each step.
The figure gets laterally inverted and the number of arrows increases by one in each step.
In each step, the figure gets vertically inverted and a line segment is added to the RHS-end.
Similar figure repeats in every second step.
Similar figure repeats in every second step and each time a particular figure reappears, it gets rotated through 90°.
The circle moves sequentially one, two, three, four, ... spaces (each space is equal to half-a-side of the square boundary) in anti-clockwise direction.
Similar figure repeats in every second step and each time a particular figure reappears, it gets rotated through 180°.
Similar figure repeats in every second step. Each time a particular figure reappears, it gets rotated through 180° and the number of arrowheads increases by one.
The arrow moves 4, 3, 2 and 1 spaces clockwise sequentially and the pin moves 5, 4, 3, and 2 spaces anti-clockwise sequentially.
A new small line segment is added to one of the lines in the figure and this addition takes place sequentially in anti-clockwise direction.
All the elements move one space clockwise and two spaces clockwise alternately.
As we go from left to right, the arrow moves 45° and 90° anti-clockwise alternately and the pin moves 45° and 90° clockwise alternately.
There is an addition of four lines in the pre-existing figure in each of the successive figures.
The figure rotates 45° and 90° alternatively in anti-clockwise direction and half leaf is added everytime to the figure.
